title: Multipart Commands, Part Ledgers, and Cleanup · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs multipart commands, part ledgers, and cleanup from browser, Node, TypeScript, AWS SDK v3, S3, CloudFront, security, billing, and stakeholder evidence and challenges one compile-time misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for typed multipart state machine after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, choose single versus multipart transfer and part size from object size, part-count limits, memory, network, retry, and completion constraints.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, persist upload ID, part number, offset, size, checksum, ETag, attempt, and owner so completion uses the exact accepted part list.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, bound part workers and bytes, cancel safely, drain results, preserve completed evidence, and prevent async task or buffer leaks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Multipart Threshold Part Size · changed case · Review Multipart Thre…
steps.instruction:
review case — A review TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 4275 during a regional recovery and budget exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate readonly multipart part evidence and AbortSignal-driven cleanup; do not treat TypeScript types, a resolved promise, CORS, ETag, or an emulator as stronger proof than it is; then change region, retrieval, or transfer cost and defend the revised budget action. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, choose single versus multipart transfer and part size from object size, part-count limits, memory, network, retry, and completion constraints.

Which action demonstrates Multipart Threshold Part Size without dropping earlier contracts?
